Title: godot-lib — the companion library
Summary: A small Godot 4 / C# plugin you drop into your project's addons/ folder. Provides 5 base classes that handle the 'walk a folder of .tres, index by key' pattern, plus a manifest emitter that writes bleepforge_manifest.json on editor load.
Body:
## What it is

`godot-lib` is the Godot side of Bleepforge — a small companion plugin that ships in the [godot-lib/](https://github.com/ymoraviadev-droid/bleepforge/tree/main/godot-lib) folder of the Bleepforge monorepo. Drop its `addons/bleepforge/` into your Godot project's `addons/` folder and enable it in Project Settings → Plugins.

What it provides:

- **5 C# base classes** that handle the registry pattern most Godot projects write by hand for each domain — walk a folder of `.tres`, index by key, expose O(1) lookup, opt into editor-time hot reload via `[Tool]`. Subclass with your concrete resource type, override 2-4 hooks, register as a Godot autoload, you're done.
- **A manifest emitter** ([the Bleepforge manifest contract](/help/under-the-hood/manifest-contract)) that reflects over your `BleepforgeResource` subclasses + registries on every editor load and writes `bleepforge_manifest.json` at your project root.
- **`Tools → Re-export Bleepforge manifest`** menu item for manual re-emit.

Using godot-lib is **opt-in**. Existing Bleepforge users without it still get the seven hardcoded game-domain editors (Items, Quests, NPCs, etc.) exactly as before.

## The 5 base classes

| Class | Manifest kind | Identity |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `BleepforgeResource` | (marker base for all) | — |
| `BleepforgeRegistry<T>` | `domain` | Field on the resource (e.g. `Slug`, `Id`) |
| `BleepforgeFolderedRegistry<T>` | `foldered` | Composite `<folder>/<basename>` from path |
| `BleepforgeDiscriminatedRegistry<TBase>` | `discriminatedFamily` | Field on the base (variants loaded by Godot's ResourceLoader) |
| `BleepforgeEnumRegistry<TEnum, T>` | `enumKeyed` | Enum-typed field on the resource |

Each base class has a worked example in its XML-doc summary. Typical subclass shape:

```csharp
[Tool]
public partial class ItemRegistry : BleepforgeRegistry<ItemData>
{
    public static ItemRegistry Instance { get; private set; } = null!;
    
    protected override string GetFolder() => "res://world/items/";
    protected override string GetKey(ItemData item, string resPath) => item.Slug;
    
    public override void _EnterTree()
    {
        Instance = this;
        base._EnterTree();
    }
}
```

Add `ItemRegistry` to your project's autoloads (Project Settings → Autoload). At runtime, `ItemRegistry.Instance.Get("some_slug")` is your access point.

## Installation

1. Copy `godot-lib/addons/bleepforge/` into your Godot project's `addons/` directory.
2. Open your project in the Godot editor (4.4+, tested on 4.6).
3. **Build C# first.** Click the hammer icon (top-right) or `Project → Tools → C# → Build Project`. Wait for "Build successful" in the Output panel.
4. `Project → Project Settings → Plugins` → enable **Bleepforge**.
5. The library writes `bleepforge_manifest.json` at your project root immediately.
6. Point Bleepforge at your Godot project (sync mode). The Manifest tab in [Diagnostics](/diagnostics/manifest) shows the parse result.

> **Why step 3 matters:** Godot's plugin loader runs before the project's C# build by default. If you skip the manual build on first install, Godot will try to enable Bleepforge against an empty assembly, fail with `Unable to load addon script from path: 'res://addons/bleepforge/BleepforgePlugin.cs'`, and silently disable the plugin. After step 3 builds the assembly, step 4's enable works on first try. **Subsequent project opens work normally** — the build runs alongside the editor and the plugin loads cleanly. This is a Godot 4 C# addon platform limitation; every C# addon hits it on fresh install.

## Editor-time hot reload

When your registry subclass has `[Tool]` AND is added as an autoload, the registry runs in the Godot editor too. The base class connects to `EditorInterface.Singleton.GetResourceFilesystem().FilesystemChanged` — when a `.tres` in the registry's folder changes (you edit it in Godot OR Bleepforge writes to it), the registry rebuilds and emits a `RegistryRebuilt` signal.

At runtime (game running, not in editor), there's no automatic hot reload. Call `Rebuild()` manually if you need it, or restart the game. Polling-based runtime reload is a future concern.

## License

**godot-lib is Apache License 2.0** — permissive for adoption. Drop it into your project without thinking about license compatibility.

**The Bleepforge editor itself is GNU Affero General Public License v3 or later** — copyleft. The split is deliberate: maximize library adoption, prevent rebadge-and-monetize forks of the editor.

See [TRADEMARK.md](https://github.com/ymoraviadev-droid/bleepforge/blob/main/TRADEMARK.md) — forks are welcome under the licenses, but use a different name.

## See also

- [The Bleepforge manifest contract](/help/under-the-hood/manifest-contract) — the JSON schema godot-lib emits.
- The [godot-lib README](https://github.com/ymoraviadev-droid/bleepforge/blob/main/godot-lib/README.md) on GitHub — full installation walkthrough + the 5 base classes' XML-doc.
- The [test-project README](https://github.com/ymoraviadev-droid/bleepforge/blob/main/godot-lib/test-project/README.md) — synthetic Godot project that exercises all 4 entry kinds end-to-end. Good reference if you want to see real subclass shapes.
